reasoning: The recall involves a very small amount of highly perishable product (303 pounds of chicken caesar wraps) that had "Best By" dates (Feb 21-25) which already expired around the time the recall was issued (Feb 24). This means the physical product recovery and disposal process will conclude almost immediately. However, the exact resolution criteria depend on the USDA FSIS officially changing the recall status from "Active" to "Closed" on their website. The administrative process for the recalling firm to submit final documentation and for FSIS to review, conduct effectiveness checks, and formally close the recall typically takes several months. While the very small scope and short shelf life might expedite things, 97 days (late February to June 1) is a relatively short timeframe for resolving government bureaucratic processes. I estimate a 38% probability that it closes before the June 1, 2025 deadline.
